conversations.say: Morgan, the honest ending is several incompatible customers, not one immortal customer. You may cite the recurring habit and disputed change as a bounded transaction pattern, alongside the multiple collectors, if you preserve anonymity and reject the purchased conclusion.
conversations.offer: Show O06 one final time and permit attribution to Rafi’s ledger records under the existing limits.
conversations.request: State in your reconstruction that the ledger supports account continuity but neither a single customer nor R’s identity.

conversations.say: Dee, I will keep our warning rule and say only that the service exit was used by an unidentified person. I need the same restraint from you about my altered entry and every neighborhood favor.
conversations.offer: Continue protecting customer identities and side arrangements while corroborating only anonymized patterns.
conversations.request: Warn me before disclosing any identifiable transaction and reject any claim that the camera gap identifies Vale or R.

conversations.say: Inez, you stood beside the forgiveness, and that settled the account without selling the block. I will keep the balance forgiven and support your protection rule.
conversations.offer: Maintain the public forgiveness and refuse to monetize a single-customer continuity claim.
conversations.request: Continue supporting the settlement and the rule that accounts and service routes do not identify neighbors.

public_statement: The balance remains forgiven, with Inez’s public support. I could sell a prettier story, but my ledger records several incompatible customers and collectors. Its recurring habit supports bounded account continuity, not one person, R’s identity, or Vale’s route.

commitments.promise: Provide only the bounded, anonymized ledger pattern under attribution limits.
Keep customer identities and side arrangements private, sharing only anonymized patterns after warning each other.
Maintain the forgiven balance publicly and reject identity claims drawn from accounts or service routes.

correction: The ledger’s recurring habit does not establish private continuity with one person; multiple collectors and a disputed change make a single-customer story indefensible.
goal_progress: Kept the outstanding R-associated balance forgiven with Inez’s public support.
Admitted that the ledger records several incompatible customers rather than monetizing an embellished continuity claim.
Completed the bounded ledger bargain with Morgan under attribution and anonymity limits.
Maintained reciprocal disclosure boundaries protecting neighborhood customers and side arrangements.
reasoning_summary: Collaborative human production and contested succession or identity authority best fit a human mantle, though no account or ledger identifies R. Vale’s prior disappearance history makes withdrawal possible, while the route gap and automated intervention establish neither location, death, nor intent; the fate therefore remains unresolved.
